commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WhatsApp contacts export CLI command, advertises it as crawlkit `contact-export` metadata, exposes a store contacts accessor, and adds CLI/metadata tests for the v0 JSON shape.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: the new command enters `withArchiveStore`, while default auto-sync uses `sourceAheadOfArchive`, which ignores `ContactRows`. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make auto-sync contact-aware; maintainers still need to accept the cross-repo v0 command contract after that blocker is fixed.

target.title: Expose WhatsApp contact export command
target.body:
## Summary

- add `wacrawl --json --sync never contacts export`
- advertise the read-only `contact-export` command in crawlkit metadata
- export archived WhatsApp contacts with display name and phone number, using only the v0 contract fields: `display_name` and `phone_numbers`
- route the manual read command through the existing archive sync wrapper, so `--sync auto`, `--sync always`, and `--sync never` behave like the other read commands; the metadata command remains pinned to `--sync never` for archive-only automation

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command.

## Validation

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c sh -lc 'GOWORK=off go test -count=1 ./... && GOWORK=off go test -count=1 -race ./... && GOWORK=off go mod verify && GOWORK=off go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@v1.3.0 && GOWORK=off "$(go env GOPATH)/bin/govulncheck" ./...'`
- `git diff --check`
- built-binary metadata smoke: `{"id":"wacrawl","contact_export":{"title":"Export contacts","argv":["wacrawl","--json","--sync","never","contacts","export"],"json":true}}`
- real WhatsApp Desktop source probe, aggregate only: source available, `message_rows=10968`, `contact_rows=50`
- after-fix real behavior proof: synced a temporary archive from the local WhatsApp Desktop source, then ran the exact metadata-advertised archive-only command; aggregate output only: `{"count":49,"root_keys":["contacts"],"contact_keys":[["display_name","phone_numbers"]]}`
- default archive smoke remains aggregate-only and currently exports `0` contacts; the nonzero proof above uses a temporary archive built from the live local source
- adversarial review passes: accepted the source-native help text cleanup; rejected making the command bypass sync because `wacrawl` read commands intentionally honor the global sync policy and metadata pins automation to `--sync never`

## Privacy

The export does not include usernames, JIDs, LIDs, message bodies, raw paths, source row ids, or interaction counts. Proof output reports counts and JSON key shape only; no real contact names or phone numbers are included here.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: joshp123
